Aloe Vera.watering: Water deeply but infrequently. Allow soil to dry completely between waterings.
Aloe Vera.sunlight: Bright, direct sunlight (6+ hours per day).
Aloe Vera.tips: Avoid getting water on the leaves to prevent rot. Best for dry climates.

Areca Palm.watering: Keep soil consistently moist but not soggy. Use filtered water if possible.
Areca Palm.sunlight: Bright, indirect light. Direct sun can scorch leaves.
Areca Palm.soil: Rich, slightly acidic potting mix with good drainage.
Areca Palm.tips: Enjoys high humidity. Mist leaves regularly in dry indoor environments.

Chinese Money Plant.watering: Water when the top inch of soil feels dry. Drooping leaves mean it's thirsty.
Chinese Money Plant.sunlight: Bright, indirect light. Rotate weekly for even growth.
Chinese Money Plant.tips: Propagates easily from 'pups' that sprout from the base.

English Ivy.watering: Keep soil moist but not wet. Let top half-inch dry out in winter.
English Ivy.sunlight: Medium to bright indirect light. Can tolerate lower light.
English Ivy.tips: Great for hanging baskets or climbing. Wash leaves occasionally to remove dust.

Jade Plant.watering: Allow soil to dry out almost completely before watering. Very drought tolerant.
Jade Plant.sunlight: Full sun to bright indirect light.
Jade Plant.tips: Symbol of good luck. Can live for decades with proper care.

Monstera Deliciosa.watering: Water every 1-2 weeks, allowing soil to dry halfway between waterings.
Monstera Deliciosa.sunlight: Bright, indirect sunlight. Can adapt to medium light.
Monstera Deliciosa.soil: Peat-based potting mix with perlite for aeration.
Monstera Deliciosa.tips: Wipe large leaves with a damp cloth to help it 'breathe' better.

Peace Lily.watering: Keep soil consistently moist. It will 'droop' visibly when it needs water.
Peace Lily.sunlight: Low to medium indirect light. Direct sun will burn the leaves.
Peace Lily.soil: High-quality potting soil that retains some moisture.
Peace Lily.tips: One of the best air purifiers. Keep away from drafts.

Pothos.watering: Allow top inch of soil to dry out between waterings. Very forgiving.
Pothos.sunlight: Adaptable to low or bright indirect light.
Pothos.tips: Extremely hard to kill. Perfect for beginners and busy plant parents.

Rubber Plant.watering: Water when top 2 inches of soil are dry. Reduce watering in winter.
Rubber Plant.sunlight: Bright, indirect light. Variegated types need more light.
Rubber Plant.tips: Avoid cold drafts and sudden temperature changes.

Snake Plant.watering: Allow soil to dry out completely. Water every 2-6 weeks depending on season.
Snake Plant.sunlight: Very adaptable; thrives in low to bright indirect light.
Snake Plant.tips: Converts CO2 to oxygen at night. Excellent for bedrooms.

African Violet.watering: Keep soil moist but never soggy. Avoid getting water on leaves.
African Violet.sunlight: Bright, indirect light. Avoid direct midday sun.
African Violet.soil: Special African Violet mix or peat-based soil.
African Violet.tips: Water from the bottom by placing the pot in a saucer of water for 30 mins.

Anthurium.watering: Water when the top inch of soil feels dry. Enjoys high humidity.
Anthurium.sunlight: Bright, indirect light. Lower light reduces flower production.
Anthurium.soil: Coarse, well-draining mix (like orchid bark and perlite).
Anthurium.tips: The 'flowers' are actually modified leaves called spathes.

Asparagus Fern.watering: Keep soil moist. Mist daily to maintain high humidity.
Asparagus Fern.sunlight: Bright, dappled light. Avoid direct afternoon sun.
Asparagus Fern.tips: Not a true fern. Watch out for small thorns on mature stems.

Begonia.watering: Water when top inch of soil is dry. Avoid overwatering.
Begonia.sunlight: Bright, indirect light. Some morning sun is okay.
Begonia.tips: Rotate often to keep growth balanced and even.

Bird of Paradise.watering: Keep soil moist during growth; let top 2 inches dry in winter.
Bird of Paradise.sunlight: Full sun to very bright indirect light.
Bird of Paradise.tips: Needs lots of space as it grows quite large and tall.

Birds Nest Fern.watering: Keep soil consistently moist. Water the soil, not the center 'nest'.
Birds Nest Fern.sunlight: Medium to bright indirect light. Avoid direct sun.
Birds Nest Fern.tips: High humidity is key to preventing brown leaf edges.

Boston Fern.watering: Keep soil damp at all times. Use a humidifier or mist daily.
Boston Fern.sunlight: Bright, indirect light. Can tolerate some shade.
Boston Fern.tips: Classic porch plant. Sheds leaves if it gets too dry.

Calathea.watering: Use distilled or rainwater. Keep soil evenly moist.
Calathea.sunlight: Medium indirect light. Direct sun fades leaf patterns.
Calathea.tips: Leaves move up and down during the day (Prayer plant family).

Cast Iron Plant.watering: Let soil dry halfway between waterings. Very drought tolerant.
Cast Iron Plant.sunlight: Low to medium light. Thrives in dark corners.
Cast Iron Plant.tips: Toughest indoor plant. Tolerates dust and neglect.

Chinese Evergreen.watering: Water when top 2 inches dry. Tolerates low water.
Chinese Evergreen.sunlight: Low to medium indirect light. Keep away from direct sun.
Chinese Evergreen.tips: Easy care and very resistant to common indoor pests.

Christmas Cactus.watering: Keep soil moist but not wet. Reduce water after blooming.
Christmas Cactus.sunlight: Bright, indirect light. Needs dark nights to bloom.
Christmas Cactus.tips: Can live for decades and be passed down as an heirloom.

Chrysanthemum.watering: Keep soil consistently moist. Water daily in hot weather.
Chrysanthemum.tips: Great for outdoor seasonal color and air purification.

Ctenanthe.watering: Keep soil moist. Enjoys high humidity and warm air.
Ctenanthe.sunlight: Medium indirect light. Keep away from direct sun.
Ctenanthe.tips: Wipe leaves with a damp cloth to keep them shiny.

Daffodils.watering: Water regularly during growth. Let soil dry after flowering.
Daffodils.tips: Plant bulbs in autumn for spring blooms. Deer resistant.

Dracaena.watering: Allow top 50% of soil to dry out. Sensitive to fluoride.
Dracaena.tips: Trim lower leaves as they age to maintain a clean look.

Dumb Cane.watering: Water when top inch of soil is dry. Use room temp water.
Dumb Cane.tips: Sap is irritating; wear gloves when pruning or handling.

Elephant Ear.watering: Keep soil consistently moist. Very thirsty during summer.
Elephant Ear.sunlight: Bright, indirect light or partial shade.
Elephant Ear.soil: Rich, organic-rich soil that holds moisture.
Elephant Ear.tips: Grows best in warm, humid conditions with plenty of space.

Hyacinth.watering: Keep soil moist during growth and bloom period.
Hyacinth.tips: Highly fragrant flowers. Great for spring gardens.

Iron Cross Begonia.watering: Water when top inch dry. Avoid getting leaves wet.
Iron Cross Begonia.sunlight: Bright, indirect light. Direct sun will scorch leaves.
Iron Cross Begonia.tips: Grown for its unique textured foliage and cross pattern.

Kalanchoe.watering: Allow soil to dry out completely. Very drought tolerant.
Kalanchoe.tips: Re-blooms best with long periods of darkness in winter.

Lilium.watering: Keep soil consistently moist during the bloom season.
Lilium.sunlight: Bright, indirect light or morning sun.
Lilium.tips: Very toxic to cats; keep out of reach of pets.

Lily of the Valley.watering: Keep soil moist. Thrives in cool, damp locations.
Lily of the Valley.sunlight: Partial to full shade. Avoid direct sun.
Lily of the Valley.tips: Spreads easily as a groundcover. Very fragrant.

Money Tree.watering: Water when top 2 inches dry. Prefers deep waterings.
Money Tree.sunlight: Bright, indirect light. Rotate for even growth.
Money Tree.tips: Avoid moving it frequently; it prefers a stable spot.

Orchid.watering: Water about once a week. Let water run through the bark.
Orchid.sunlight: Bright, indirect light (east-facing window is ideal).
Orchid.soil: Special orchid bark or moss; never standard soil.
Orchid.tips: Needs good air circulation around the roots.

Parlor Palm.watering: Water when top inch of soil feels dry. Forgiving of neglect.
Parlor Palm.sunlight: Low to medium light. Tolerates very low light well.
Parlor Palm.tips: Perfect for offices or dark corners of the home.

Poinsettia.watering: Water when soil feels dry to the touch. Don't let it sit in water.
Poinsettia.sunlight: Bright, indirect light (6 hours a day).
Poinsettia.tips: Sensitive to temperature drops and cold windows.

Polka Dot Plant.watering: Keep soil moist. It will wilt noticeably when thirsty.
Polka Dot Plant.sunlight: Bright, indirect light for best color intensity.
Polka Dot Plant.tips: Pinch back stems to keep the plant bushy and full.

Ponytail Palm.watering: Water every 2-3 weeks. Store water in its bulbous base.
Ponytail Palm.sunlight: Bright, direct sunlight is best but tolerates less.
Ponytail Palm.tips: Not a true palm; actually related to Agaves.

Prayer Plant.watering: Keep soil moist but not soggy. Use lukewarm water.
Prayer Plant.sunlight: Bright, indirect light. Leaves curl in too much sun.
Prayer Plant.soil: Peat-based potting mix that holds moisture.
Prayer Plant.tips: Mist leaves or use a pebble tray to boost humidity.

Rattlesnake Plant.watering: Keep soil moist but never wet. Use filtered water.
Rattlesnake Plant.tips: Features beautiful wavy-edged leaves with dark spots.

Sago Palm.watering: Allow top 2 inches of soil to dry. Drought tolerant.
Sago Palm.sunlight: Bright, indirect light or some direct morning sun.
Sago Palm.tips: Very slow-growing. All parts are toxic if ingested.

Schefflera.watering: Water when top inch dry. Tolerates occasional dryness.
Schefflera.sunlight: Bright, indirect light. Can adapt to medium light.
Schefflera.tips: Prune back if it gets too leggy to encourage bushy growth.

Tradescantia.watering: Keep soil moist. Let top inch dry between waterings.
Tradescantia.sunlight: Bright, indirect light for vibrant purple color.
Tradescantia.tips: Grows very fast and is easy to propagate in water.

Tulip.watering: Water regularly during growth and flowering.
Tulip.tips: Best treated as an annual in warmer climates.

Venus Flytrap.watering: Use ONLY distilled or rainwater. Keep soil wet.
Venus Flytrap.soil: Pure peat moss and perlite/sand; NO fertilizer.
Venus Flytrap.tips: Do not trigger traps for fun; it wastes the plant's energy.

Yucca.watering: Allow soil to dry out significantly. Very drought hardy.
Yucca.sunlight: Full sun to very bright indirect light.
Yucca.tips: Requires very little maintenance once established.

ZZ Plant (Library).watering: Allow soil to dry out completely. Water once a month.
ZZ Plant (Library).sunlight: Low to bright indirect light. Very adaptable.
ZZ Plant (Library).tips: Thrives on neglect. Perfect for areas with no windows.
